brief_document.title: Remote-worker protocol wire shape (register/task/result/heartbeat, bidi)
brief_document.purpose: Define the wire contract for out-of-process Tier-3 activity workers — the protocol every worker SDK (cluster AR) generates stubs from. This is the bidirectional-streaming gRPC service: workers register, the server pushes tasks, workers report results and heartbeats.
brief_document.task: Add worker.proto declaring a bidirectional-streaming gRPC service with RegisterWorker, ActivityTask, ActivityResult, and Heartbeat messages. ActivityTask carries the activity type, input Payload, and the WorkflowId/ActivityId correlation; ActivityResult carries a result Payload or an ActivityError; Heartbeat carries liveness and optional progress; RegisterWorker carries the worker's namespace and the activity types it implements. Provide serde types and assert lossless round-trip. This brief defines the protocol; the server-side endpoint is AW-011/AW-012.

requirements.title: Bidirectional worker service
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a gRPC service in worker.proto using bidirectional streaming, where the server pushes ActivityTask messages down the stream and the worker pushes ActivityResult and Heartbeat messages up it, opened by a RegisterWorker handshake.
acceptance: worker.proto defines a bidirectional-streaming RPC carrying a server->worker message (ActivityTask) and a worker->server message (RegisterWorker, ActivityResult, Heartbeat) as the two stream directions.
The transport is gRPC bidi streaming, not unary polling (per D7); the proto reflects push dispatch.
tonic-build generates the streaming service and the crate compiles.

requirements.title: Task, result, and heartbeat payloads
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define ActivityTask carrying activity type, input Payload, and WorkflowId/ActivityId correlation; ActivityResult carrying a result Payload or an ActivityError; and Heartbeat carrying liveness and optional progress.
acceptance: ActivityTask carries the activity type string, the input Payload, the owning WorkflowId, and the ActivityId for correlation.
ActivityResult carries either a success result Payload or an ActivityError (using aion-core's ActivityError shape via the AW-002 conversion, preserving the retryable/terminal classification).
Heartbeat carries the WorkflowId/ActivityId it concerns and an optional progress Payload.
A unit test round-trips ActivityTask, ActivityResult (both success and error), and Heartbeat through serde and proto encode/decode.

requirements.title: Worker registration advertisement
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define RegisterWorker carrying the worker's namespace and the list of activity types it implements.
acceptance: RegisterWorker carries a namespace and a repeated activity-type field.
RegisterWorker has a serde type deriving Serialize + Deserialize and round-trips losslessly.
The proto documents that registration scopes the worker to one namespace (server-side partitioning per D8 is AW-011).

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the server-side worker registry, dispatch, or heartbeat handling — AW-011, AW-012.
SHALL NOT build any worker client — that is cluster AR.
SHALL NOT define how the engine surfaces a Tier-3 activity need — that is AE; this brief defines only the wire protocol.
verification: No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.

checklist.text: worker.proto defines a bidirectional-streaming service with RegisterWorker, ActivityTask, ActivityResult, and Heartbeat messages.

ActivityTask carries the activity type, input Payload, and the WorkflowId/ActivityId correlation; ActivityResult carries a result Payload or an ActivityError; Heartbeat carries liveness and optional progress.
RegisterWorker carries the worker's namespace and the activity types it implements.

stories.text: As a worker SDK author, I want the worker protocol defined in the shared contract so that registration, task dispatch, results, and heartbeats are identical across every language.
As a remote worker, I want to connect once, advertise the activity types I implement, and have tasks pushed to me so that I run heavy work without polling.

constraints.text: No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] lint bypasses per CLAUDE.md; tests needing a running server gate at runtime (a *_TEST_URL env var with a logged skip), never #[ignore].
lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
aion-proto is a leaf crate: it depends on aion-core and external crates only — no aion, no aion-server, no store backend. Structural; must hold (per D2).
aion-server depends on aion, aion-proto, and a store backend among workspace crates; it contains no workflow execution logic — it delegates every durable decision to the Engine (per D5).
Wire data types (proto messages, streamed events, summaries) derive Serialize + Deserialize; config types that carry secrets are Deserialize-only (D4, project credential policy).
Library errors use thiserror (WireError in proto, ServerError in server); anyhow appears only in aion-server's top-level main. No unwrap/expect in library code; lock/stream poison handled explicitly.
No cross-namespace operation reaches the engine: namespace authorisation and scoping happen at the adapter boundary before any Engine call (per D8).
A slow or stalled WebSocket consumer is dropped with a typed lag error; per-connection buffers are bounded; the engine broadcast is never back-pressured by a subscriber (per D6).
No hardcoded operational defaults (ports, heartbeat window, buffer bound, timeouts): all from config; engine-owned defaults deferred to the engine (per CLAUDE.md 'no assumed defaults').
The wire Event/WorkflowFilter/WorkflowSummary/WorkflowStatus and ID types are aion-core's types, not re-declared wire clones (per D2).

resolved_context.intention: Build the network skin of Aion. The aion engine crate is transport-agnostic by design — no HTTP, gRPC, or WebSocket — so a CLI embeds it with nothing but a file-backed store. Teams running many services want a managed deployment: one binary clients and workers point at, that streams live workflow events to a dashboard, isolates tenants, and dispatches activity tasks to out-of-process workers in any language. That is aion-server, and the wire contract it speaks is aion-proto. aion-proto is a pure leaf-level contract — gRPC service definitions plus serde wire types — that the server, every client SDK (AL), and every worker SDK (AR) agree on, defined once so they never drift. aion-server is the deployable: it wraps a live Engine, translates wire requests into Engine calls, tails the engine's event broadcast to feed WebSocket subscribers, runs the server side of the remote-worker protocol, partitions everything by namespace, and serves the dashboard's API and static assets. It adds no execution logic — every durable decision still happens in aion.
